name: Combo Multiplier
description: A scoring layer that increases rewards for consecutive successful rhythm judgments and resets or reduces value when misses, breaks, or invalid inputs occur.
design_notes: Combo multipliers turn consistency into score pressure. The design should define which hit judgments increment combo_count, which states break it, how multipliers scale, how dropped inputs are displayed, and whether assists or calibration changes mark the score differently.
design_purpose: Reward sustained rhythmic accuracy beyond individual note hits.
Create tension by making a single miss affect score potential.
Support rank, achievements, feedback, and end-of-song summaries.
player_fantasy: The player feels momentum build as accurate hits stack into a high-value streak.

parameters.name: combo_count
parameters.description: Current successful streak count after judgment processing.

parameters.name: combo_break_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that determines which events reset the streak.

parameters.name: multiplier_tier_id
parameters.description: Current reward tier derived from combo_count thresholds.

parameters.name: streak_threshold_table_id
parameters.description: Data table mapping combo bands to multiplier tiers.

parameters.name: hit_judgment_id
parameters.description: Judgment result used to update combo and score state.

parameters.name: recovery_grace_note_count
parameters.description: Optional assist allowance before a streak fully breaks.

parameters.name: score_submission_id
parameters.description: Result identifier used for replay validation and leaderboards.

edge_cases: A simultaneous chord has one perfect note and one missed lane input.
A hold note starts successfully but releases early during an otherwise valid streak.
A pause or disconnect occurs after a hit but before combo feedback displays.
Assist grace prevents a break locally but ranked score submission must mark the assist.
Replay validation sees the same inputs but a different note order after chart migration.
common_bugs: Combo increments per visual effect instead of per committed hit_judgment_id.
Misses break combo twice for one note because both miss cleanup and input failure emit events.
Multiplier tier updates after score calculation, applying the old tier to the wrong note.
End-of-song summaries include hidden assist grace but leaderboards submit as unassisted.
Combo UI resets on scene reload while score state still contains the previous streak.
balancing_notes: High multipliers make full-combo play exciting but can make early mistakes feel unrecoverable.
Grace systems are useful for accessibility and casual modes when they are surfaced honestly in scoring.
Streak thresholds should reflect chart density; sparse songs need different pacing than dense charts.
accessibility_notes: Allow reduced visual clutter for combo effects while keeping essential streak state visible.
Offer assist profiles that soften combo breaks without hiding the scoring difference.
Use text and shape changes, not only color or screen shake, to show combo state.

implementation_notes.unity: Update combo_count from structured judgment events emitted by the rhythm subsystem, then calculate multiplier tier before adding note score. Store score_submission_id, assist flags, and chart version for leaderboard and replay validation.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep combo state in a scoring node that receives hit_judgment_id events in deterministic chart order. UI nodes should subscribe to combo changes instead of maintaining their own streak counters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a scoring component or subsystem to own combo_count, break rules, and multiplier tiers. Networked or ranked modes should submit authoritative result data with chart_id, score_submission_id, and assist metadata.
implementation_notes.web: Process combo updates in the same reducer that handles timing judgments. Persist final result records with combo break events so refreshes, replays, and leaderboard submissions cannot reinterpret the streak.

relationships.reason: Combo multiplier needs hit judgments or beat matches to determine whether the streak continues.
Hit markers or timing labels make combo gain and break events readable.
Achievements can reward long streaks without changing scoring rules.

example_games.title: Guitar Hero
example_games.note: Uses note streaks and multipliers as a central scoring layer.

example_games.title: Thumper
example_games.note: Uses rhythmic accuracy and streak pressure in score-focused play.
